message:
feat(control_channel): replace !token.is_empty() auth with transcript-bound HMAC capability verification (bd-3cvu)

The control channel authenticated traffic with !token.is_empty(), which
accepts any non-empty string as a valid credential, directly contradicting
the three-kernel trust model.

Replace the bare auth_token with a ChannelCredential struct containing
subject_id, epoch, nonce, and an HMAC-SHA256 MAC that binds:
  channel_id || subject_id || audience || direction || sequence_number ||
  payload_hash || epoch || nonce
using length-prefixed encoding and a domain-separated transcript prefix.

Key changes:
- Epoch-scoped HMAC key derivation via derive_epoch_key()
- Constant-time MAC comparison via ct_eq_bytes()
- Per-epoch nonce tracking (bounded to 8192) for cross-message replay
  detection
- ChannelConfig gains channel_id and audience fields
- ChannelError::AuthFailed gains a reason field and retryable() method
- Audit entries gain subject_id, epoch, and reason_code fields
- sign_channel_message() public API for credential issuance

Add 8 adversarial security tests: guessed-token injection, payload swap,
cross-channel replay, stale-epoch reuse, wrong-direction replay,
wrong-secret rejection, sequence-number tamper, and nonce reuse. Add
regression test explicitly verifying that a 32-byte non-empty but
cryptographically invalid MAC is rejected.

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with latest tracking status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
